reasoning: The informal statement "the last two digits of 3^(3^100) is 03" is mathematically equivalent to 3^(3^100) ≡ 3 (mod 100). The Lean expression `3 ^ (3 ^ 100) % 100 = 3` parses (since `^` binds tighter than `%`) as `(3 ^ (3 ^ 100)) % 100 = 3`, which at the default type ℕ is exactly this modular claim. Since 3^(3^100) ≫ 100, asserting `... % 100 = 3` indeed says the decimal expansion ends in "03". No hypotheses are added or omitted, and the types are correct, so the formalization is strictly faithful.
